name: Mie Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the verdant nature and farming traditions of Mie

description: A green field charged at the centre with a white emblem stylising the kanji 三 ('three') of the prefecture's name.
history: Mie's prefectural symbol was adopted on 23 December 1964. The flag, with the white emblem on a green field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the lush forests and pearl-rich seas of the Ise-Shima coast

a stylised abstraction of the kanji 三 (mi, 'three') of 三重 (Mie), the three horizontal strokes set within a circle representing harmony, with a flowing form evoking pearls of Ago Bay and the development of the prefecture.
